text: This article attempts to unravel possible ways of bringing about reconciliation in South Africa by searching for linkages across culturally and religiously unique worldviews of the majority of South Africans. The humanising idea of Ubuntu will be examined and juxtaposed with the principles of fellowship and service as they emerge in Christian living, to explore their relevance across worldviews. This paper makes a comparative study of these principles drawing linkages from the unique Ubuntu perspective and its correspondence to the principles of fellowship and service within the framework of the New Testament teachings. Such a correspondence between unique African culture and biblical principles could yield a cohesive approach towards enhancing national reconciliation.
